observations.recommendation: We recommended and reiterated to Management that the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the collectors must stop immediately the practice of holding large amount of undeposited/unremitted collections and strictly follow the rule that collections must be deposited intact and in full amount, daily, the next banking day, at least once a week, or as soon as the collections reach ₱5,000.00, as applicable.

We recommended that all Accountable Officers be required to prepare a weekly Report of Collection and Deposit (RCD) whether there is any collection and remittance/deposit, coincident with the actual date of collections and properly record the same in their respective cashbooks, for proper and timely recording also in the books by the Accounting Office.
We recommended that Management undertake periodic monitoring of cash accountabilities of all the Accountable Officers and consider increasing the bonds, if necessary.

We recommended and reiterated that Management:
Strictly comply with the rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances in accordance with the provisions provided under COA Circular No. 97-002;
Issue demand letters to officials and employees, who have outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2021, especially those who are still in the active service, copy of which to be submitted to the Auditor with proof of service and dated to enforce settlement of unliquidated CAs; and
File request for authority to write-off dormant or long outstanding unliquidated CAs, if necessary, to the Office of the Auditor, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management:
Strictly adhere to the above cited provisions on the procurement of infrastructure projects. Henceforth, all perfected contracts together with its supporting documents must be submitted for review and evaluation to the Office of the Auditor within five working days from execution of contract;
Direct concerned officials to ensure that procurement for infrastructure projects is properly supported with complete documentation; and
Require the Accountant to impose 10 percent withholding for every progress payment made to the contractor as retention money.

We recommended that Management
Ensure that all the changes in the PPAs are incorporated in a supplemental or revised Annual Investment Plan and that development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and attainable within the desired time frame in order to maximize allocated funds to have an immediate impact on the socio-economic development of the LGU; and
Strictly adhere to the provisions of the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in formulating PPAs in order to achieve the maximum benefits for its constituents.

We recommended that Management:
Draw a check from the General Fund to transfer the unexpended current and prior years’ LDRRMF balance to Special Trust Fund in compliance with Section 21 of RA 10121;
Require the MDRRMO to include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in the preparation of LDRRMF Investment Plan; and
Require the Budget, Accounting and MDRRM Offices to reconcile their records on the utilization of LDRRMF including utilization of continuing and unexpended prior years’ appropriation and make adjustment when necessary, to reflect the true amount utilized for LDRRMF.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Sagguniang Bayan to enact an ordinance authorizing supplemental appropriation for savings realized for the discontinuance of project for PRDP Counterpart-Pinamalatican to Sta Maria Farm to Market Road with Bridge which was re-appropriated to other PPAs;
Submit explanation/justification for the deficiencies noted; and
Strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 2.6 of the 2016 Budget Operations Manual for Local Government Units.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer immediately submit the subject CY 2021 DVs with complete supporting documents, collections accounts and necessary monthly reports including the lacking DVs for the current year and prior years. Otherwise, the subject disbursements will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that purchases be properly recorded in the logbook registries of AF before it issuance and refrain from issuing new AF to an AO without requiring the presentation of Report of Collection and Deposit and determining  that previously issued AF are reported and remitted in full.

We recommended that Management require from the concerned elected official to liquidate immediately the cash advance and stop the practice of granting elected officials cash advances for purposes other than for official traveling expenses and instead designate a special disbursing officer to handle/facilitate payment of various operating expenses.

We recommended that Management:
Immediately submit the DVs with complete and required supporting documents for the subject cash advances;
Require the LCE to immediately liquidate the cash advances and submit the required documents to the Intelligence and Confidential Fund Audit Unit of COA, copy furnished the Audit Team of the duly received transmittal letter; and
Limit CA granted for confidential fund to the requirement for 3 months.

We recommended that Management
Require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to immediately and fully liquidate the subject cash advances;
Stop the granting of cash advances that is not for a legally specific purpose and limit the grant thereof to those provided for in Item 3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and other pertinent and applicable rules and regulations on cash advances; and
Record cash advances to the appropriate cash advance account in accordance with the purpose for which it was made. Prepare adjustments, if applicable.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management:
Demand immediate settlement/liquidation of all the outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2020 pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002;
Require the Municipal Accountant, as the LGU’s internal auditor, to strictly monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances and avoid granting additional CAs unless the previous cash advance is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made pursuant to Section 89 of PD No. 1445 to avoid accumulation of cash advances; and
Cause the recovery of unliquidated cash advances for those who have retired and/or separated from government service, and to file the request for write-off for the unliquidated CAs that has been outstanding for long period of time; and
Institute appropriate administrative and/or legal action against the accountable officer/s for the immediate settlement these cash advances.

We recommended that Management require:
Require the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count to determine the existing and serviceable insurable properties of the LGU;
Require the General Services Office (i) to submit an inventory list of insurable properties; and (ii) to secure insurance of all its insurable property from the GSIS in order to mitigate possible losses on the part of the Municipality; and
Ensure that sufficient amount is included in the budget to cover for the property insurance premiums.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer exert efforts to reconcile the reciprocal Due to LGUs and Due from LGUs accounts between the Municipality and the Province and Barangays.
A journal entry voucher must be prepared to take up in the books of the Municipality any adjustments noted during the reconciliation process.

We recommended the following to Management:
Adherence to BIR RR No. 2-98 on the withholding and remittance, intact, in full amount, and within the prescribed period of taxes due to the national government; and
Require the concerned officials to trace existence of unreconciled balances, reconcile the same and immediately remit the correct payables to enable the government to allocate resources for priority development programs.

We recommended that Management:
Remit fully and timely the amount withheld from its employees to GSIS, PAG-IBIG and PhilHealth to avoid incurring any interest or penalties due to delayed remittances;
Ascertain the details of unremitted prior year/s balances, including abnormal balances, through reconciliation of their records with the GSIS, PAG-IBIG and PhilHealth and remit immediately the adjusted balance to the respective agency and effect the necessary adjustment/s in the books of accounts; and
Require the Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for the account of GSIS, PAG-IBIG and PhilHealth.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop approving the payment of honoraria to the members of the abovementioned councils unless sufficiently and properly supported by legal basis for the grant of said honorarium. Otherwise, the payments amounting to ₱58,400 during CY 2020 and subsequent payments may be considered for further audit action as audit suspension or disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all depreciable assets are provided with depreciation and draw a journal entry voucher to take up unrecorded current and prior years’ depreciation expense to presently fairly the financial statements in accordance with IPSAS 17.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the In Charge of the Office (ICO)-Municipal Treasurer immediately submit the subject CY 2020 DVs with complete supporting documents, collections accounts and necessary monthly reports including the lacking DVs for CY 2019 and CY 2020. Otherwise, the subject disbursements will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that copies of perfected contracts, purchase orders irrespective of amount, and each and every supporting document forming part thereof, shall be properly filled up and within five working days from issuance thereof, be submitted to the Auditor concerned for purposes of conducting timely auditorial review and evaluation of said contracts and purchase orders.

We recommended that the LCE, Local Sanggunian and LDC ensure that a Supplemental Investment Program and Supplemental Annual Procurement Plan be prepared for BGCM COVID-19-related PPAs by strictly complying with the regular budget process.
The Local Sanggunian must also enact an ordinance to create a special account in the General Fund and instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a special account for the BGCM and other COVID-19 funds, as mandated by law and required by sound financial management.

We recommend that Management ensure:
To utilize the Bayanihan Grant and implement COVID-19-related PPAs strictly in accordance with the approved budget and supplemental investment plan/program; and
That all disbursements for COVID-19 PPAs must pass through the Municipal Budget Office, as are appropriate, for the determination of the existence/availability of funds or appropriation.

We recommended that Management:
Submit Sangguniang Bayan Resolution authorizing the grant of COVID-19 hazard pay;
Require the HRMO, Municipal Accountant and the concerned personnel to submit, if any, the required (i) DTRs; (ii) Authority of the subject employees to physically report for work during the period of ECQ implementation; (iii) copy of the duties and responsibilities of Casual, Contractual and Job Order personnel during the period for which the grant was paid; (iv) Accomplishment Reports to show that their services were urgently necessary in the delivery of public service during the implementation of the ECQ in the Municipality; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to show proof that the amount of additional hazard pay (COVID-19) paid to the personnel who are already entitled to Hazard Pay, Hazardous Duty Pay, Hazard Allowance or other similar benefits under existing laws, issuances, rules, and regulation, is the correct and proper amount in accordance with DBM BC No. 2020-1.

We recommended that Management:
Submit Sangguniang Bayan Resolution authorizing the grant of special risk allowance;
Require the HRMO, Municipal Accountant and the concerned personnel to submit, if any, the required (i) DTRs; (ii) Authority of the subject employees to physically report for work during the period of ECQ implementation; (iii) copy of the duties and responsibilities of Casual, Contractual and Job Order personnel during the period for which the grant was paid; (iv) Accomplishment Reports to show that their services were urgently necessary in the delivery of public service during the implementation of the ECQ in the Municipality; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to show proof that the amount of SRA paid to LGU personnel, in relation to its COVID-19 response, is the correct and proper amount in accordance with DBM BC No. 2020-2.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Exert effort to validate/establish the recorded amount of Receivable accounts;
Require all Municipal personnel with outstanding cash advances who are about to retire, resign or transfer to other agency to settle first their cash and property accountabilities before granting them the necessary clearance; and
Request for write-off of dormant account receivable, unliquidated cash advances and fund transfer accounts which remained non-moving for 10 years or more and where settlement/collectability could no longer be ascertained in accordance with the guidelines and procedure provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 for fair presentation of the Financial Statements.
observations.reason: Management did not start yet the validation of the recorded dormant receivable accounts, and require all Municipal personnel to settle and/or refund the cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management revert back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund the unutilized/unexpended DRRMF amounting to ₱469,223 that remained outstanding in the Special Trust Account for more than 5 years as required under Section 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-02 to be made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the local sanggunian.
observations.reason: The subject reports were still not able to revert back the unexpended LDRRMF in the trust account which remained outstanding for more than 5 years.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF based on the approved AIP to support disaster risk management activities following the policies and procedures on the utilization of the LDRRMF under paragraph 6.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
Also, we reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management instruct the LDRRMO to submit the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-02, to facilitate timely verification and audit thereof.
observations.managementAction: Management was still not informed of the utilization of the CY 2019 LDRRMF, thru the monthly report of sources and utilization.
observations.reason: The Audit Team was not informed of the utilization of the CY 2019 LDRRMF, thru the monthly report of sources and utilization.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management’s adherence to the accounting and reporting guidelines for the accounting and reporting guidelines for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) set forth in COA Circular No. 2012-002, with emphasis on the following:
The Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) must see to it that in the preparation of the budget proposal for the ensuing year, the approved LDRRMFIP must be the basis in making the current year appropriations for QRF, MF-MOOE and MF-CO. The unexpended balance of the MF-CO for the previous year must be forwarded and made as continuing appropriation, retained as to amount and specific program or project. When asked as to the basis of said continuing appropriation, the previous LDRRMFIP/s must be presented showing the portion of projects for capital outlays. While the report/data on the unexpended balance of QRF and MF-MOOE for the current year must be forwarded to the Accounting Office at the end of the year for appropriate action to transfer said amount to the Special Trust Fund;
The Municipal Accountant, in drawing the disbursement voucher to effect the transfer, must properly validate and verify the correctness of the reported unexpended balance by the MBO to ensure that there would be no excess or insufficiency of fund transfer. The unexpended balances of QRF and MF-MOOE at year end, lodged in the Special Trust Fund and specifically segregated into the year it was obtained, must be reported and communicated to the LDRRMO for the preparation of the Investment Plan. Further, it is advised that correcting entries be effected on Trust Liabilities-DRRM for the amount of understatement in the account; and
The LDRRMO must prepare LDRRMFIP annually composed of the plan for the current year to be forwarded to the MBO and for the previous years’ unexpended balance of QRF and MF-MOOE based on the report received from the Accounting Office. The LDRRMFIP must include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended DRRMF of previous years.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer’s respective for CY 2018 was still not reconciled. No reports for CY 2019 were yet submitted by the MDRRMO, Accounting and Budget.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management judiciously plan the programs/projects/activities for the Solid Waste Management, allocate amounts per PPA listed and promptly implement them as embodied in the 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan.
Also, we recommended that the Municipality prioritize the corrections of the 10-year SWM Plan and submit it to the EMB Regional Office V and NSWC for approval, as required under Section 16 of RA 9003 to ensure the efficient management of solid waste generated within its jurisdiction.
observations.reason: Management was still not able to take the necessary action to implement the suggested recommendations. The Audit Team was also not informed if there was any action thereon.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Solid Waste Management Board to require the other 24 barangays of the Municipality to allocate a certain parcel of land through their Sanggunian for the establishment of MRF. The Management must also exert its best efforts towards full implementation of relevant environmental laws.
Further, we reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipality cause the timely closure of the LGU’s existing dumpsite and find ways and means of generating fund to pursue the construction of a sanitary landfill.
The 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan must periodically be reviewed to include the timetable for the construction of sanitary landfill as required by law.
observations.reason: Management was still not able to take the necessary action to implement the suggested recommendations. The Audit Team was also not informed if there was any action thereon.

Stop the practice of allowing reimbursement of expenses unless made under extreme emergency and urgent operating requirements that do not involve huge sums of money;
Ensure that payments for supplies and materials as well as other operating expenses be made directly to the suppliers; and
Comply with the requirements of adequate procurement planning in accordance with Section 7 of R.A. 9184 that will regulate procurement activities and discourage reimbursement of expenses.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management –
Refrain from utilizing the Special Education Fund for non-related programs/projects. The utilized amounts for non-related expenses must be restituted to the fund in order not to deprive the stakeholders of the benefits they may derive therefrom; and
Require Local School Board to utilize the SEF for the priority and allowable programs, projects and activities enumerated in Sections 100 (c) and 272 of the Local Government Code and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017 to ensure the proper utilization of the fund.
observations.reason: Management was still not able to take the necessary action to implement the suggested recommendations. The Audit Team was also not informed if there was any action thereon.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
In the succeeding year’s preparation of Annual Gender and Development Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report, the schedule in GAD Planning and Budgeting expressly stated in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01must be observed to conform with the law and regulations governing GAD;
Submit a copy of the Annual Gender and Development Plan and Budget and the corresponding Accomplishment Report to COA within the prescribed period for the timely audit so that recommended corrective measures may also be issued in the appropriate time; and
Establish and maintain GAD database to serve as basis in the identification of priority gender issues that are crucial for the Municipality’s evidence-based planning and policy making.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Management to require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare and submit to the GPPB the procurement monitoring report within the time frame as stipulated in Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184, so that monitoring and evaluation of the LGUs compliance to the provisions of the Procurement Act can be made. Moreover, we also suggested to furnish our office a copy of such report for information and reference in audit.
observations.reason: The Audit Team was not informed if there was any action thereon.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC and the concerned LGU’s offices/units to:
Prepare the Annual Procurement Plan and the respective Project Procurement Management Plans in accordance with the procedure and format provided in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and the Government Procurement Manuals; and
Conduct the procurement activities strictly in accordance with the duly approved APP and PPMPs and the procurement laws, rules and regulations.
This is without prejudice to any suspension or disallowance that may be issued on the procurements for CY 2018 which have not been covered and included in the approved APPs.
observations.reason: The Audit Team was not informed if there was any action thereon.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
The Local Finance Committee or the Municipal Treasurer, Budget Officer and Municipal Planning Officer formulate a detailed income projection based on Statistics of past years’ experienced and profile of local taxpayers for close monitoring and thorough evaluation of the performance of each revenue account;
Also, devise effective plan to avoid, mitigate and control the risks of possible non-collection of each projected revenue account and to upgrade the income forecasting of the Municipality; and
Periodic review of each projected revenue account must be made by concerned municipal officials to ascertain whether the estimated income is based on appropriate projection to provide optimum public services to the public; and
The Municipal Treasurer must conduct periodic review to check the collection performance level and devise remedial measures for each revenue account that will improve more the collections of taxes, fees and charges that registered good results.
observations.reason: The Audit Team was not informed if there was any action thereon.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LGU thru the Municipal Treasurer’s Office conduct inspection of the business establishments that are not included in the database of the Municipality to actually validate the existence of the business establishments.
Require the immediate imposition of business taxes, fees and charges and explanation from owners/proprietors on operating without the benefit of business permits. When necessary and in accordance with the provision of the Revenue Code of the Municipality and the Local Government Code and its IRR, file appropriate charges against business establishments operating without the benefit of permits as required.
We also suggested that the Municipal Treasurer strictly enforce the requirement of Section 152 of the LGC where barangay clearances are to be submitted by business establishments before business taxes are paid and permits are issued.
observations.reason: The Audit Team was not informed if there was any action thereon.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
That the Municipality, through the Municipal Treasurer strictly follow the procedural guidelines prescribed in the Revenue Code and the IRR of the Local Government Code for the termination or retirement of business;
That the Municipality strictly implement the provision of Section 171 of the Local Government Code to ensure the accuracy of business tax collected and penalize erring business taxpayers by requiring them to pay tax deficiencies including applicable interests and surcharges; and
Collect the taxes due from these business establishments, including any interest, surcharges or penalties for non-payment or delay in payment.
observations.reason: The Audit Team was not informed if there was any action thereon.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Treasury Office and all its collectors strictly implement the provision of Section 13(2) of the Local Revenue Code by requiring CTC taxpayers to file a sworn declaration of total earnings derived from business, compensation, exercise of profession and income from real property during the preceding year. The sworn declaration must be the basis for assessing the amount of CTC tax.
observations.reason: The Audit Team was not informed if there was any action thereon.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following-
The Treasury Office strictly enforced the provision of Section 156, Article 28 of Local Revenue Code particularly on the Special Permit and Regulatory Fees on selling/serving liquors throughout the Municipality by conducting inspection on all business entities within its jurisdiction to ensure that stores and commercial entities selling liquors are strictly regulated. The inspection may be conducted by requesting the aide of barangay officials and law enforcement personnel;
Institute administrative and criminal sanctions against those commercial entities found violating the provisions of the Local Revenue code; and
Increase the rate of Special Permit and Regulatory Fees on selling liquors and other sin-products through an ordinance, to discourage the sale, disposition and consumption thereof in order to ensure the health, peace and comfort of the inhabitants.
observations.reason: The Audit Team was not informed if there was any action thereon.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: 
Require the recipients of counterpart cash or construction materials to submit utilization reports and/or status of implementation of the programs, projects or activities to which these are intended for to ensure that these are indeed exclusively used for the intended public purpose of the Municipality and to negate the possibility of being used for personal or private purpose; and
Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 on the Rules and Regulations in the Grant, Utilization and Liquidation of Funds Transferred to Implementing Agencies, particularly the responsibilities of the source agency and the implementing agency.
observations.reason: The LGU still did not require utilization reports and/or status of implementation of PPAs from the recipients of fund transfers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant transfer to the LDRRM Trust Fund the entire unexpended balance of the LDRRMF. Henceforth, ensure that the unexpended balance of the 30% QRF and MOOE under the 70% Mitigation Fund be transferred to the LDRRM Special Trust Fund at the end of each year. Further, it is advised that correcting entries must be effected on Trust Liabilities-DRRM for the amount of understatement in the account.
observations.reason: The unexpended balance of the LDRRMF which remained outstanding was still not totally transferred to the Trust Liabilities-DRRM account as per results of CY 2018 audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the LDRRMO to submit the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to the aforecited Circular, to facilitate timely verification and audit thereof.
observations.reason: The subject reports were still not prepared and submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality cause the timely closure of the LGU’s existing dumpsite and find ways and means of generating fund to pursue the construction of a sanitary landfill.
The 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan must periodically be reviewed to include the timetable for the construction of sanitary landfill as required by law.
observations.managementAction: The LGU still utilizes their existing dumpsite.
observations.reason: The municipality still utilizes their existing dumpsite and had not taken management action.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the In Charge of the Office (ICO)-Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit the Disbursement Vouchers with complete supporting documents and other lacking CY 2019 accounts for our verification and post-audit, otherwise, we will be compelled to issue the corresponding suspensions/disallowances on the subject disbursements.

Strict compliance with the timelines on the recording and submission of accounts to the Auditor, within the first 10 days of the ensuing month, as required by the abovementioned existing law, rules and regulations, is once again emphasized.

Management and the concerned officials and employees must be reminded of the administrative or criminal liability, under Section 39 (1) (3) of PD No. 1445, Section 23 (1) (3) of the Administrative Code of 1987 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code, which may be imposed as a consequence of its inability or refusal to render accounts without justifiable cause. Management must initiate necessary administrative and/or criminal action in case of unjustified failure/refusal to effect compliance with the foregoing requirements by subordinate officials.

We recommended and reiterated to Management that:
The ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the collectors must stop immediately the practice of holding large amount of undeposited collections;
The three collectors found to have unremitted collections as of June 30, 2019 must have them deposited immediately intact and in full with the authorized government depository bank; and
Institute appropriate administrative and/or legal actions against the AOs for non-compliance with the above-cited regulations, if warranted.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare and submit the liquidation reports with complete supporting documents relative to the utilization of trust funds received from these NGAs. It must be assured that proper reporting and accounting is made on the utilization of the fund transfers to fairly present financial transactions at year-end.

We recommended that Management continue its commitment to faithfully remit trust collections, however, the current administration still has to exert effort in reconciling and establishing the unremitted trust collections from prior years’ transactions and once established, accordingly remit the same to the respective government agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert effort to identify the sources of the difference between the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts through reconciliation and effect immediately the adjustments upon verification. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant must also determine if the purposes for which the transfers between funds were made have already been achieved or completed, hence must be liquidated and recorded in the separate books of accounts to present fairly the balances of the said accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Local Finance Committee or the Municipal Treasurer, Budget Officer and Municipal Planning Officer to formulate a detailed income projection based on Statistics of past years’ experience and profile of local taxpayers for close monitoring and thorough evaluation of the performance of each revenue account. Also, devise effective plan to avoid, mitigate and control the risks of possible non-collection of each projected revenue account and to upgrade the income forecasting of the Municipality;
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to undertake a review of the present budgeting done under the stewardship of the Municipal Budget Office to set a clearer guide for the concerned revenue generating Departments toward attaining the targets set; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer and other Departments involved in generating revenues to meet its targets by conducting periodic review of collection performance by the concerned officials to ascertain whether collection targets are attained; and to analyze the reasons for the shortfall in collections and find alternative solution to minimize, reduce or avoid incurrence of shortfall in the future.
We recommended that the Local School Board Council incorporate in its budget programs and activities intended for ECCD as required under Section 7 (b) of R.A. No. 10410.

We recommended that Management stop payments thru reimbursement process in the acquisition of supplies, materials and other miscellaneous expenses, except petty purchases to be paid out of the Petty Cash Fund and to adhere strictly to the policies mandated under RA No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-003

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal officials and employees concerned to strictly comply with the mandatory submission of perfected contracts and its supporting documents to COA including approved purchase orders (POs), not only for those under the 20% Development Fund but including all contracts entered into and POs issued by the Municipality, for a timely and complete conduct of its auditorial and technical review; and,
Submit immediately all perfected contracts with complete supporting documents for review, the results of which will be used in post-audit of disbursements, otherwise, notices of suspension will be issued to enforce compliance.

We reiterated, with amendments, the following recommendations to Management:
Require the Municipal Engineer, the MPDO and the Municipal Accountant to submit the list of all on-going projects and those that are to be implemented during the year including the Quarterly Reports on Publicized Government PPAs which are already due for submission; and
In the succeeding year’s preparation of the said reports, must be strictly observed.

We recommended that the BAC Chairman, its members, including the BAC Secretariat and the Technical Working Group must conduct all its bidding procedures strictly in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR, to avoid incurring deficiencies that may affect the legality and propriety of related financial transactions.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee post the Notices of Awards and Notices to Proceed on the procurements of goods and infrastructure projects in the PhilGEPS website in compliance with the provisions of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to cause the transfer of the amount of ₱10.92 million representing the unexpended balances of LDRRMF for CY 2019 and prior years to the special trust fund account;
Require the Municipal Accountant the following:
Draw a Journal Entry Voucher upon transfer of the fund to correct the understatement by the same amount of both the Transfers of Unspent Current Year DRRMFs to the Trust Funds and Trust Liability-DRRMF accounts;
Establish the existence and propriety of the balance of Continuing Appropriation for Capital Outlay amounting to ₱4.44 million. Otherwise, make necessary actions to transfer the same as unexpended MF-MOOE from prior years to the Special Trust Account pursuant to DBM- DILG- NDRRMC JMC No. 2013-1 and COA Circular No. 2012-02; and
Review the Aging of Payables under Trust Fund of the FS as of December 31, 2019 and make adjustments, as appropriate for the fair presentation of accounts in the FS.
Require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget and MDRRMO to conduct annual reconciliation of their respective records on the utilization of LDRRMF to avoid inconsistent balances on their reports and ensure that discrepancies noted are immediately investigated and adjusted thereon accordingly.
We further recommended the utilization of the LDRRMF for projects, programs and activities that would mitigate and reduce the impact and/or effect of calamities and adversities that may befall the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRM Officer and Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to initiate the creation and filling up of the mandatory positions prescribed under the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC 2014-1 to properly institutionalize the organization of the LDRRMO and to have continuity in service and implementation of its programs.

We recommended that Management refrain from charging to GAD Budget those expenditures that were not concerned with gender issues and activities and not part of the GAD’s Plan. This is without prejudice to our further audit action regarding the suspensions/disallowances that may be issued on the subject disbursements.

Stop the granting of cash incentives and allowances which are not in conformity with laws, instead the LGU may grant the payment to its RHU personnel for “On Call Pay” for those placed on “On Call” status to respond to urgent or immediate need for health/medical assistance and “Remote Assignment Allowance” for those who accept assignment in remote areas or isolated station, both equivalent to 50 percent of basic pay pursuant to Sections 15 and 22 of RA No. 7305; and,
Refrain from collecting birthing facility fee from birthing patients in the absence of law authorizing such charges.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop approving the payment of honoraria to the members of the MPOC and MADAC unless sufficient legal basis for the grant of said honorarium has been submitted to the Audit Team for further evaluation of the matter on hand. Otherwise, the payments amounting to ₱72,000.00 during CY 2019 and subsequent payments may be considered for further audit action as audit suspension or disallowance.

We recommended that:
The Municipality:
Require the concerned accountable officer to restitute immediately the full amount of unremitted collections, and institute appropriate administrative and/or criminal action against them;
Refrain those collectors found to have committed shortages, lapping of collections and delay in remittance from acting as revenue collectors.
Replace them with trustworthy personnel having high virtue on accountability for better fiscal control in the treasury; and
Consider the designation of a liquidating officer in order to facilitate the verification and remittance process.
The Municipal Treasurer:
Instruct all collectors to prepare RCDs and remit all collections to the Municipal Treasurer at the close of each business day in accordance with Section 29, Vol. I of NGAS Manual. In case of collectors assigned to the field, where travel time from their places of assignment to the treasurer’s office is more than one day, turnover of collections must be made at least once a week or as soon as the collection reach ₱5,000 as prescribed under Section 26 of COA Circular No. 92-382;
Be more prudent in the acknowledgment of remittances in terms of accuracy of the amount and correct serial numbers of accountable forms;
Refrain from allowing incomplete remittances from the collecting officers; and
Set control measures to avoid or minimize delayed remittances of collections and institute disciplinary action to concerned accountable employees under his supervision, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: Some accountable officers found to have undeposited collections during cash count have partially restituted the amount.
observations.reason: Some accountable officers found to have undeposited collections still failed to fully restitute the amount.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Management,
Demand immediate settlement/liquidation of the outstanding cash advances of the OIC-Municipal Treasurer pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002;
Require the Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances and avoid granting additional CAs unless the previous cash advance is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made pursuant to Section 89 of PD No. 1445; and
Institute appropriate administrative and/or legal action against the accountable officer for the immediate settlement of these cash advances. Otherwise, we will be compelled to exhaust the said legal remedies for violating Article 217 and 218 of the Revised Penal Code and Section 89 of P.D. 1445 which is punishable under Section 128 of the same Code.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to send demand letters to officials and employees with existing unliquidated cash advances and make them settle such obligations within the year.
observations.reason: The Management committed to send demand letters to officials and employees with existing unliquidated cash advances and make them settle such obligations within the year, but was still not able to take such action as of this report date.

observations.managementAction: The BAC was able to schedule early the procurement activities for some planned PPAs.
observations.reason: There is still inadequate planning, budgeting and utilization of the 20 percent DF and there is still inclusion of non-allowable PPAs. Moreover, out of ₱21.90 million appropriation, only ₱14.64 million was utilized during CY 2018.

observations.recommendation:

27.) observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant establish and maintain special accounts, complete subsidiary ledgers, books of accounts and prepare separate financial statements and/or financial reports to account for the operations of each economic enterprise for the consumption of the LGU and other users of these information to assess the performance of each LEE and for decision making directed towards financial self-sufficiency.

observations.recommendation:

The Municipal Treasurer exert efforts in maintaining an updated cashbooks for the Cash In Bank accounts. Require the Municipal Accountant and concerned Focal Persons, as regards the fund transfers, to immediately liquidate disbursements therefrom for the implementation of the programs/projects/activities for which they were intended, to properly reflect the unliquidated amount that would correspond with the available balance of cash in bank for these trust collections.

Submit immediately all perfected contracts with complete supporting documents for review, the results of which will be used in post-audit of disbursements, otherwise, notices of suspension will be issued to enforce compliance.

to require the other 24 barangays of the Municipality to allocate a  certain
